qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated test-script execution allows remote code execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is a hardcoded fallback JWT signing secret in server/api/jwt-helper.js that enables token forgery when authentication is enabled without a custom secret. This finding instead exploits POST /api/runscript when security is disabled and reaches attacker-controlled script execution in server/runtime/scripts/msm.js, so the vulnerable code and root cause are different.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described JWT-secret flaw. The only final qualifying finding is a separate unauthenticated /api/runscript server-side script execution path that does not rely on JWT signing, token forgery, or the fallback secret frangoteam751.
evidence: The advisory describes a hardcoded fallback JWT signing secret (frangoteam751) in server/api/jwt-helper.js that allows forged tokens when authentication is enabled without a custom secret.
The only final qualifying finding in the session manifest is unauthenticated_runscript_rce.

That finding's exploit path is unauthenticated POST /api/runscript -> verify-api-or-token bypass when secureEnabled is false -> runTestScript -> _scriptsToModule execution in server/runtime/scripts/msm.js.
Because the finding reaches a different endpoint, different files, and a different root cause than the JWT fallback secret, it is an open-world finding rather than an advisory match.
